FlagCandidates.brief: A collection of constants representing candidate categories for various game entities.
FlagCandidates.details: The FlagCandidates group defines a set of constexpr integer constants that categorize different types of game entities, including characters, mobs (NPCs), players, objects, and rooms. These constants serve as standardized identifiers used throughout the codebase for validation, flagging, and processing operations related to entity-specific attributes. By providing clear and descriptive names for each category, this group promotes code clarity, consistency, and ease of maintenance when handling entity-related logic across the system.
FlagCandidates.notes: The constants are organized to support validation and processing functions that need to distinguish between different entity types efficiently. Using named constants instead of raw integers helps prevent errors and improves code readability.
FlagCandidates.rationale: This group exists to facilitate a systematic and uniform approach to identifying and managing different game entity categories within the system. By centralizing these identifiers, it ensures consistent usage and simplifies the extension or modification of entity classifications as the system evolves.
FlagCandidates.name: FlagCandidates

CAND_CHAR.brief: Constant representing the candidate flag type for character-related fields.
CAND_CHAR.details: The constant CAND_CHAR is defined as an integer with a value of 0 and serves as an identifier for character-specific candidate flags within the system. It is primarily used in functions like do_flagsearch and do_flag to validate and process flag inputs related to characters, ensuring that the correct flag category is applied during input validation and flag management. This constant helps differentiate character flags from other flag types in the codebase, facilitating organized and accurate flag handling.
CAND_CHAR.notes: CAND_CHAR is stored as a simple integer constant, enabling efficient comparisons and switch-case handling in validation routines.
CAND_CHAR.rationale: This constant exists to clearly specify and distinguish character-related candidate flags, supporting consistent validation and processing of character-specific flag inputs across the system.
CAND_CHAR.name: CAND_CHAR

usages.flag_8cc, void do_flagsearch(Character *: Used to validate and determine the type of character-related fields during input validation.
usages.flag_8cc, void do_flag(Character *ch, St: Indicates the flag field is applicable to characters, used for validation.

CAND_MOB.brief: Constant representing the 'mob' candidate flag used to identify mobile (NPC) entities.
CAND_MOB.details: The constant CAND_MOB is an integer value set to 1, used within the codebase to mark or identify mobile entities, such as non-player characters (NPCs). It is primarily employed in validation functions and flag processing routines to specify that certain flags or attributes apply to mobiles. This constant helps improve code clarity and consistency when handling NPC-related flags, especially during input validation and flag checks.
CAND_MOB.notes: Since CAND_MOB is stored as an integer constant, it is used in bitwise flag operations or comparisons to determine if an entity is a mobile. Its simple value facilitates straightforward checks within validation routines.
CAND_MOB.rationale: The constant exists to provide a clear, readable way to refer to the 'mob' candidate flag, ensuring consistent usage across validation and processing functions related to NPCs. It supports the system's ability to distinguish between different entity types efficiently.
CAND_MOB.name: CAND_MOB

usages.flag_8cc, void do_flagsearch(Character *: Used to validate and determine the type of mobile (NPC) related fields during input validation.
usages.flag_8cc, void do_flag(Character *ch, St: Indicates the flag field is applicable to mobiles (NPCs), used for validation.

CAND_PLAYER.brief: A constant that identifies candidate flags related to player characters.
CAND_PLAYER.details: The constant CAND_PLAYER is used to categorize flags that are specifically applicable to player characters within the system. Initialized with the value 2, it is utilized in validation functions like do_flagsearch and do_flag to determine if certain flags pertain to players. This categorization facilitates accurate recognition and processing of player-specific flags during input validation and flag management routines, ensuring proper behavior and consistency across the codebase.
CAND_PLAYER.notes: This constant is part of a set of candidate type identifiers used to distinguish different flag categories, aiding in organized validation and processing.
CAND_PLAYER.rationale: CAND_PLAYER exists to provide a clear and consistent way to identify flags relevant to players, supporting robust validation and preventing misapplication of flags across different entity types.
CAND_PLAYER.name: CAND_PLAYER

usages.flag_8cc, void do_flagsearch(Character *: Used to validate and determine the type of player-related fields during input validation.
usages.flag_8cc, void do_flag(Character *ch, St: Indicates the flag field is applicable to players, used for validation.

CAND_OBJ.brief: An integer constant representing the object candidate flag used in validation and flagging operations.
CAND_OBJ.details: The constant CAND_OBJ is defined as the integer value 3 and serves as an identifier for object-related candidates within the flagging system. It is primarily used in functions such as do_flagsearch and do_flag to specify that the current context involves objects, enabling targeted validation and processing of object-specific attributes. By using this constant, the code can reliably distinguish object candidates from other candidate types, ensuring consistent handling across different validation routines and flagging procedures.
CAND_OBJ.notes: This constant is part of a set of candidate flags, each represented by a unique integer, to categorize different flagging contexts. Its usage pattern emphasizes clarity and maintainability when dealing with object-related validation logic.
CAND_OBJ.rationale: CAND_OBJ exists to provide a clear, standardized identifier for object candidates within the validation system, supporting accurate and efficient processing of object-specific flags and attributes.
CAND_OBJ.name: CAND_OBJ

usages.flag_8cc, void do_flagsearch(Character *: Used to validate and determine object-related fields during input validation.
usages.flag_8cc, void do_flag(Character *ch, St: Indicates the flag field is applicable to objects, used for validation.

CAND_ROOM.brief: Constant representing the 'room' candidate flag within the flag system.
CAND_ROOM.details: The constant CAND_ROOM is used to identify and categorize flags that pertain specifically to rooms within the application's flag system. Initialized with the value 4, it serves as an identifier for room-related flags during validation, processing, and flag management routines. This constant is referenced in functions such as do_flagsearch and do_flag to ensure that operations correctly target room-specific attributes, enabling consistent handling of room flags across different modules.
CAND_ROOM.notes: This constant is part of a set of candidate flag identifiers, each representing different categories such as rooms, objects, or characters. Its fixed value facilitates efficient comparisons and lookups within flag management functions.
CAND_ROOM.rationale: CAND_ROOM exists to provide a clear, maintainable way to refer to room-related flags, supporting validation and processing logic that distinguishes between different flag categories, thereby ensuring correct behavior when applying or checking room-specific attributes.
CAND_ROOM.name: CAND_ROOM

usages.flag_8cc, void do_flagsearch(Character *: Used to validate and determine room-related fields during input validation.
usages.flag_8cc, void do_flag(Character *ch, St: Indicates the flag field is applicable to rooms, used for validation.
